context: Displaying a confirmation for a destructive action (delete record)
rationale: Modals block all other interaction forcing user focus; prevent accidental confirmation; easier to dismiss with Escape key
tradeoffs: Disruptive to workflow; can create modal fatigue if overused; avoid for non-destructive confirmations

context: Navigation on mobile with 4-6 top-level destinations
rationale: Thumb reachability zone on modern phones; matches platform conventions (iOS/Android); visually persistent status
tradeoffs: Eats 56px of vertical space; not suitable for >6 items; desktop equivalent needed (sidebar or top nav)

context: Long multi-step form (5+ fields) vs compact form
decision: Use stepped/wizard form with progress indicator
rationale: Reduces cognitive load per step; progress indicator motivates completion; easier to validate per step; reduces abandonment
tradeoffs: More clicks; user cannot see full form at once; back-navigation must be explicitly implemented

context: Showing detailed settings or configuration options
decision: Use a right-side drawer/sheet instead of modal
rationale: Drawers allow more vertical space for complex forms; user can reference underlying content; feels less disruptive
tradeoffs: Less urgency than modal; dismiss on backdrop click can lose data; mobile needs careful height management

context: Displaying a list of 10+ filterable data rows
decision: Use a data table with column sorting and filters
rationale: Tables enable comparison across rows; column sorting + filter covers >80% of user needs without custom search; familiar pattern
tradeoffs: High implementation cost; poor on mobile (need responsive strategy: cards or horizontal scroll); requires pagination strategy

context: Onboarding a new user to a complex product
decision: Use contextual tooltips + empty-state prompts instead of modal walkthrough
rationale: Users skip modal tours 80% of time; contextual hints appear when relevant; empty states guide action at point of need
tradeoffs: Requires more feature flags and state tracking; harder to test; power users may find hints patronizing (add dismiss)

context: Showing system status or background process progress
decision: Use a toast notification (non-blocking) for success; blocking progress bar only for mandatory wait
rationale: Non-blocking toasts let users continue working; blocking UI only justified when action cannot be undone mid-process
tradeoffs: Toasts disappear before user reads them (min 4s); do not use toast for errors requiring action; stack management needed

context: User needs to select one option from 3-5 mutually exclusive choices
decision: Use segmented control / radio group; avoid dropdown
rationale: Dropdowns hide options requiring extra click; segmented controls show all options at once; reduce decision friction
tradeoffs: Segmented controls break at >5 options or long labels; dropdowns win for >8 options or variable label lengths

context: Displaying search results with mixed content types (articles + users + files)
decision: Use grouped search results with type icons and section headers
rationale: Grouping by type reduces cognitive scanning; users can skip irrelevant sections; section headers aid screen readers
tradeoffs: Increases result list height; sorting by relevance across groups is complex; consider tabs if type imbalance is large

context: Rich text content that needs inline editing
decision: Use click-to-edit inline pattern with visible affordance on hover
rationale: Reduces mode-switching; what-you-see-is-what-you-edit; faster for experienced users; reduces modal overhead
tradeoffs: Risk of accidental edits; must show clear edit/save/cancel states; screen readers need explicit editing mode announcement

decision: Use a pricing table with highlighted recommended plan
rationale: Visual hierarchy directs to best-value option; column comparison enables rational decision; recommended label anchors choice
tradeoffs: Risk of dark pattern if highlight is purely commercial not value-based; tables break on mobile (need stacked cards)

context: Error state after a failed form submission
decision: Show inline field-level errors + a summary banner at top of form
rationale: Field-level errors point directly to the problem; banner ensures users who scrolled past see something failed; screen readers announce banner first
tradeoffs: Two error locations to maintain; avoid duplicate message wording; clear errors immediately on correction (live validation)

decision: Use a filter panel (collapsible sidebar or drawer) with applied-filters chips
rationale: Chips show active filters at a glance; drawer prevents filter UI from dominating page; clear-all chip resets quickly
tradeoffs: Chips can overflow on many filters; need clear affordance to open filter panel; mobile needs sheet pattern

context: User needs to complete an action that takes 15+ seconds
decision: Show progress bar with estimated time + cancel option
rationale: Users abandon if no feedback within 3s; estimated time reduces anxiety; cancel option for long ops is table stakes
tradeoffs: Estimation is hard to get right; indeterminate bar is better than wrong estimate; do not show for <3s operations
